wind_info.description: Crosswind from the right, slight tailwind component.

bh.shot_strategy: RHBH exposes underside to wind. Prefer RHFH. If RHBH, throw with significant hyzer to keep the flight plate from turning over.

fh.shot_strategy: Wind from the right is manageable for a RH forehand. Focus on a clean release.

bh.shot_strategy: LHBH fade goes right, wind pushes left. They work together. LHBH is comfortable here. Consider a neutral to slightly overstable disc.

fh.shot_strategy: Wind from the right is against LHFH fade. Prefer LHBH. If LHFH, use an overstable disc with a smooth release.

wind_info.description: Crosswind from the right, slight tailwind component.

bh.shot_strategy: RHBH exposes underside to wind. Prefer RHFH. If RHBH, throw with significant hyzer to keep the flight plate from turning over.

fh.shot_strategy: Wind from the right is manageable for a RH forehand. Focus on a clean release.

bh.shot_strategy: LHBH fade goes right, wind pushes left. They work together. LHBH is comfortable here. Consider a neutral to slightly overstable disc.

fh.shot_strategy: Wind from the right is against LHFH fade. Prefer LHBH. If LHFH, use an overstable disc with a smooth release.

bh.shot_strategy: RHBH exposes underside to wind. Prefer RHFH. If RHBH, throw with significant hyzer and an overstable disc to combat wind.

fh.shot_strategy: Wind from the right is manageable for a RH forehand. Focus on a clean release and keeping the disc low.

fh.shot_strategy: Wind from the right is against LHFH fade. Prefer LHBH. If LHFH, use an overstable disc with a smooth release to fight the wind.

bh.shot_strategy: RHBH underside away from wind. Manageable with hyzer. RHFH also viable. Focus on a clean hyzer flip if needed.

fh.shot_strategy: Wind from the left is manageable for a RH forehand. Focus on a smooth, controlled release.

bh.shot_strategy: LHBH fade goes right, wind pushes right. Double trouble. Prefer LHFH. If LHBH, use an overstable disc and focus on controlling the fade.

fh.shot_strategy: Wind from the left is against LHFH fade. Prefer LHBH. If LHFH, aim slightly left and let the wind push it back.
